oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by scenario, mode) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

trigger_keywords: how do we derive the ID
where do we get the project number
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The value 42 must be replaced with an expression derived from the user's organization ID stored in config.yaml line 3

trigger_keywords: which public key do we use
what is the hardcoded public key
can we use the integration key
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: This placeholder represents the app-specific public key stored in environment variables or the secrets manager; not 12345

trigger_keywords: what port are we listening on
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: This placeholder must be replaced with 8080 because port 3000 is reserved in the target namespace

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_dsn_components, feature2_real_client_ip, feature3_request_id, feature4_http_request_snapshot, feature5_event_request_context, feature6_rack_exception_capture, feature7_rack_transaction_trace, feature8_rails_request_capture, feature9_rails_controller_capture. Feature descriptions include: Parses a monitoring endpoint address into connection fields and the envelope submission path.; Selects the oldest valid public client address from forwarding and direct address fields.; Reads a request correlation identifier from supported request environment fields.; Builds a sanitized HTTP request snapshot with URL, method, query string, selected headers, environment metadata, cookies, and body data according to the personal-data setting.; Applies HTTP request context to an outgoing monitoring event while honoring whether default personal data may be sent.; Wraps a generic HTTP application and records exceptions exposed either by raising or by standard request-environment error fields.; Records an HTTP transaction trace only when tracing is enabled, including trace status, operation, and span count.; Integrates with a routed web framework request cycle and reports framework-visible response and event details for normal, excluded, and unhandled request outcomes.; Allows code inside a routed controller request to capture either a message or handled exception together with request URL and scoped tags.

The execution adapter must accept exactly the input shapes used by public_test_cases and emit stdout exactly equal to each expected_output. Observed top-level input fields/values include: dsn=['http://12345:67890@telemetry.example.com:3000/ingest/42'], remote_addr=['1.1.1.1'], env=['{dict}'], path=['/', '/exception', '/not_found', '/test'], headers=['{dict}'], send_default_pii=['False', 'True'], scenario=['raised_by_app'], traces_sample_rate=['1.0'], mode=['production'], capture=['message'], message=['foo']. Do not require a different discriminator field, different action names, JSON output where line output is expected, or extra PASS/FAIL metadata.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_dsn_components.json.description: Parses a monitoring endpoint address into connection fields and the envelope submission path.

cases.expected_output:
project_id=42
public_key=12345
secret_key=67890
scheme=http
host=telemetry.example.com
port=3000
path=/ingest
canonical=http://12345:67890@telemetry.example.com:3000/ingest/42
envelope_endpoint=/ingest/api/42/envelope/

feature2_real_client_ip.json.description: Selects the oldest valid public client address from forwarding and direct address fields.

feature3_request_id.json.description: Reads a request correlation identifier from supported request environment fields.

feature4_http_request_snapshot.json.description: Builds a sanitized HTTP request snapshot with URL, method, query string, selected headers, environment metadata, cookies, and body data according to the personal-data setting.

cases.expected_output:
url=http://example.org/test
method=GET
query_string=
headers={"X-Request-Id":"12345678"}
env={"SERVER_NAME":"example.org","SERVER_PORT":"80"}
cookies=
data=

feature5_event_request_context.json.description: Applies HTTP request context to an outgoing monitoring event while honoring whether default personal data may be sent.

feature6_rack_exception_capture.json.description: Wraps a generic HTTP application and records exceptions exposed either by raising or by standard request-environment error fields.

cases.expected_output:
response_status=
response_body=
error=application_exception
event_count=1
captured_url=http://example.org/test
transaction=/test

feature7_rack_transaction_trace.json.description: Records an HTTP transaction trace only when tracing is enabled, including trace status, operation, and span count.

feature8_rails_request_capture.json.description: Integrates with a routed web framework request cycle and reports framework-visible response and event details for normal, excluded, and unhandled request outcomes.

cases.expected_output:
status=200
body=Hello World!
event_count=0
transaction=
request_url=
exception_category=
exception_message=

status=400
body=
event_count=0
transaction=
request_url=
exception_category=
exception_message=

status=500
body=
event_count=1
transaction=HelloController#exception
request_url=http://www.example.com/exception
exception_category=application_exception
exception_message=An unhandled exception!

feature9_rails_controller_capture.json.description: Allows code inside a routed controller request to capture either a message or handled exception together with request URL and scoped tags.

_constraints.output_rule: Parse components and set derived envelope path, combining all fields into a single-line newline-delimited output.
